title: Session 02 - Nouns Flashcards
subject: english
description: Active recall cards for nouns, noun phrases, articles, countability and work-English order vocabulary.

cards.front: What is a noun?
cards.back: A noun names a person, place, thing, group, idea, quality, action, or business concept.
cards.note: Work examples: customer, restaurant, order, quality, commission, relationship.

cards.front: What is a noun phrase?
cards.back: A noun phrase is a noun plus the words that belong with it.
cards.note: Examples: a regular weekly order; the restaurant owner; my regular customers.

cards.front: Complete: I work in ____ food service sector in ____ UK.
cards.back: I work in the food service sector in the UK.
cards.note: Use the with recognised sectors and with the UK.

cards.front: Give five common nouns from work English.
cards.note: Other useful examples: product, price, payment, supplier, relationship.

cards.front: Which is correct: I live in London / I live in the London?
cards.note: Most city names do not use the.

cards.front: Which is correct: I live in UK / I live in the UK?
cards.note: Use the with the UK and the United Kingdom.

cards.front: Is invoice a concrete noun or an abstract noun?
cards.note: You can see an invoice, print it, send it, or check it.

cards.front: Is quality a concrete noun or an abstract noun?
cards.note: Quality is an idea or condition, not a physical object.

cards.front: Make a natural compound noun: price + list
cards.note: Example: The owner asked for the new price list.

cards.front: Make a natural compound noun: product + quality
cards.note: Example: Product quality is important for restaurant customers.

cards.front: Correct: Most of my customer are Turkish restaurant.
cards.back: Most of my customers are Turkish restaurants.
cards.note: Most of my usually points to plural nouns here.

cards.front: What is the plural of delivery?
cards.note: We had two late deliveries this week.

cards.front: What is the plural of box?
cards.note: The boxes arrived damaged.

cards.front: Is customer countable or uncountable?
cards.note: A customer, two customers, many customers.

cards.front: Is information countable or uncountable?
cards.note: Say some information, not an information.

cards.front: Correct: I need an information about the order.
cards.back: I need some information about the order.
cards.note: Information is uncountable.

cards.front: When do we use a?
cards.back: Use a before one general singular countable noun with a consonant sound.
cards.note: Examples: a customer, a restaurant, a product, a system.

cards.front: When do we use an?
cards.back: Use an before one general singular countable noun with a vowel sound.
cards.note: Examples: an order, an invoice, an owner, an issue.

cards.front: Correct: I want to create regular ordering system.
cards.back: I want to create a regular ordering system.
cards.note: System is singular and countable.

cards.front: First and second mention: ___ restaurant called me. ___ restaurant wants the price list.
cards.back: A restaurant called me. The restaurant wants the price list.
cards.note: First mention: a. Known second mention: the.

cards.front: Correct generally: The restaurants need reliable suppliers.
cards.note: Use no article for general plural nouns.

cards.front: Correct generally: A quality is important.
cards.note: Use no article for general uncountable nouns.

cards.front: Find the subject: The customer placed an order.
cards.note: Ask: Who placed an order?

cards.front: Find the direct object: The customer placed an order.
cards.note: Ask: Placed what?

cards.front: Find the object of the preposition: with the customer
cards.note: It comes after the preposition with.

cards.front: Correct: the customer order
cards.note: Use apostrophe + s for most singular possessive nouns.

cards.front: Correct for many restaurants: the restaurants orders
cards.note: Regular plural nouns ending in s take only the apostrophe.

cards.front: Natural: give an order to me / place an order with me
cards.note: A customer places an order with someone.

cards.front: Complete: My commission is based ____ monthly payments.
cards.note: The fixed phrase is based on.

cards.front: Say a clean three-sentence work introduction using nouns correctly.
cards.back: I work in the food service sector. Most of my customers are Turkish restaurants. My job is to help them place regular weekly orders.
cards.note: Practise until the noun phrases feel automatic.
